message:
fix(indexer/responsiveness): harden governor startup against
spawn failure and flip compare-exchange early-return shape

Two small fixes to the `Governor::ensure_started()` path introduced
in 3f947725.

1. The compare-exchange early-return was inverted. The previous
   shape was `if is_ok() { spawn... }` with no explicit handling of
   the `is_err()` (someone-else-already-started) case — it just
   fell through and did nothing, which worked for the happy path
   but was awkward to extend and hid the spawn logic inside a
   nested block. Flipped to `if is_err() { return; }` and then
   spawn on the main path, so the "I claimed the start slot" branch
   is the body of the function instead of a nested block.

2. The spawn-failure path used to `panic!("governor thread spawn
   failed")`, which would take the whole indexer down if the host
   had hit `RLIMIT_NPROC`. That defeats the governor's conservative-
   by-default design goal. Replaced with a non-panicking recovery:
   roll back `self.started` to `false` so a later caller can retry
   when resource pressure eases, emit a `tracing::warn!` explaining
   the state ("capacity pinned at 100% until a later start
   succeeds"), and leave `current_capacity` at its initial 100. The
   indexer keeps making progress even when the governor can't
   spawn a sampler thread — exactly the contract the module-level
   doc comment already promised.

Also removed the now-unused `OnceLock` import and the
`TEST_OVERRIDE` / `set_test_capacity_pct` /
`_reserve_test_override_symbol` block. The test-time override path
was dead code: every call site consults `CASS_RESPONSIVENESS_\
DISABLE=1` through the env-var path, and the `OnceLock` plumbing
was only there to paper over an "unused" warning on non-test
builds.

No behaviour change on the happy path of 3f947725 — just a safer
spawn-failure mode and a cleaner function shape.
